fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: California Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to California - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity california, how to transfer audiologist license to california, audiologist compact california, california audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology and Hearing Aid Dispensers Board

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in California.

steps.title: Create a BreEZe Account and Submit Application
steps.description: Navigate to www.breeze.ca.gov, create a new user account if you don't have one, then log in and select 'Start a New Application'. Choose 'Audiologist' and complete the online application form, paying the $120 application fee.

steps.title: Complete Fingerprint Background Check
steps.description: Complete a Live Scan fingerprinting appointment using the Board's specific 'Request for Live Scan Service' form (BCII 8016). Ensure the results are sent electronically to the Department of Justice (DOJ) and the FBI.

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts and Supporting Documents
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your qualifying educational institution to be sent directly to the Board. Also submit a Certificate of Completion of Supervised Professional Practice and any required professional references or verifications of licensure from other states.

steps.title: Pass the National Examination
steps.description: Register for and pass the national examination in Audiology as required by the Board (e.g., PRAXIS exam for Audiology). Official scores must be sent directly to the Board.

steps.title: Pay Initial License Fee
steps.description: Once your application, background check, and examination results are reviewed and approved by the Board, you will be notified to pay the $100 initial license fee through the BreEZe system.

steps.title: Receive Your License
steps.description: Upon payment of the initial license fee, your Audiologist license will be issued and viewable on your BreEZe account. You may then begin practicing in California.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a California audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does California have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in California. To work in California, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to California?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in California while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in California
hero.dek: Audiologist licensing in California is handled through the BreEZe online system, where applicants can submit their application, track its status, and manage their license.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Go to www.breeze.ca.gov and click on the 'New User' button to register for an account. Follow the instructions to create your BreEZe account. Once registered, log in and select 'Start a New Application' from the BreEZe homepage, then search for 'Audiologist' to begin your application.
